title: Lawful Processing
description: A person may process personal data only in accordance with the Act and for a lawful purpose, with consent or for certain legitimate uses.

title: Notice to Data Principal
description: Every request for consent must be accompanied or preceded by a notice informing the Data Principal of personal data and purpose, manner to exercise rights, and how to complain to the Board.

title: Notice for Pre-existing Consent
description: Where consent was given before commencement, the Data Fiduciary must give notice as soon as reasonably practicable, and may continue processing until consent is withdrawn.
deadline: As soon as reasonably practicable after commencement
notes: "As soon as reasonably practicable" is not precisely defined; may be ambiguous.

title: Option for Language of Notice
description: Data Fiduciary must give the Data Principal the option to access the notice in English or any language in the Eighth Schedule to the Constitution.

title: Valid Consent
description: Consent must be free, specific, informed, unconditional, unambiguous, with a clear affirmative action, limited to necessary personal data.

title: Clear Language for Consent Request
description: Every consent request must be in clear and plain language, with option to access in English or Eighth Schedule language, and contact details of Data Protection Officer or authorised person.

title: Right to Withdraw Consent
description: Data Principal has the right to withdraw consent at any time, with ease comparable to giving consent.

title: Cessation of Processing on Withdrawal
description: Upon withdrawal of consent, Data Fiduciary must cease and cause Data Processors to cease processing within a reasonable time, unless required or authorised otherwise.

title: Consent Manager
description: Data Principal may give, manage, review or withdraw consent through a Consent Manager. Consent Manager must be registered with the Board and accountable to Data Principal.
notes: Detailed obligations of Consent Manager in Rules - First Schedule Part B.

title: Burden of Proof for Consent
description: In proceedings, Data Fiduciary must prove that notice was given and consent obtained in accordance with the Act and rules.

title: Certain Legitimate Uses
description: Data Fiduciary may process personal data for specified legitimate uses without consent, including voluntary provision, state benefits, legal obligations, medical emergencies, employment, etc., subject to conditions.

title: Responsibility of Data Fiduciary
description: Data Fiduciary is responsible for compliance with the Act regardless of any agreement to the contrary or failure of Data Principal.

title: Contract with Data Processor
description: Data Fiduciary may engage a Data Processor only under a valid contract.

title: Completeness, Accuracy and Consistency
description: Where personal data is used to make a decision affecting the Data Principal or is disclosed to another Data Fiduciary, the Data Fiduciary must ensure completeness, accuracy and consistency.

title: Technical and Organisational Measures
description: Data Fiduciary must implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure effective observance of the Act.

title: Reasonable Security Safeguards
description: Data Fiduciary must protect personal data by taking reasonable security safeguards to prevent personal data breach.

title: Intimation of Personal Data Breach
description: In the event of a personal data breach, Data Fiduciary must give intimation to the Board and each affected Data Principal in such form and manner as prescribed.
deadline: Upon becoming aware: to Board without delay (initial) and within 72 hours (detailed). To Data Principal without delay.

title: Erasure of Personal Data
description: Data Fiduciary must erase personal data upon withdrawal of consent or when specified purpose is no longer being served, unless retention is required by law. Also cause Data Processor to erase.
deadline: Upon withdrawal or as soon as reasonable to assume purpose no longer served
notes: "As soon as it is reasonable to assume" is ambiguous.

title: Deemed No Longer Served
description: Specified purpose is deemed no longer served if Data Principal does not approach the Data Fiduciary or exercise rights for a prescribed time period.
notes: Time periods in Third Schedule of Rules.

title: Publishing Contact Information
description: Data Fiduciary must publish business contact information of Data Protection Officer (if applicable) or a person able to answer questions about processing.

title: Grievance Redressal Mechanism
description: Data Fiduciary must establish an effective mechanism to redress grievances of Data Principals.

title: Verifiable Consent for Child's Data
description: Before processing personal data of a child, Data Fiduciary must obtain verifiable consent of the parent in such manner as prescribed.

title: No Detrimental Effect on Child
description: Data Fiduciary shall not undertake processing likely to cause detrimental effect on the well-being of a child.

title: Restrictions on Child's Data Processing
description: Data Fiduciary shall not undertake tracking or behavioural monitoring of children or targeted advertising directed at children.

title: Appoint Data Protection Officer (SDF)
description: Significant Data Fiduciary must appoint a Data Protection Officer based in India, responsible to the Board of Directors, and point of contact for grievance redressal.

title: Appoint Independent Data Auditor (SDF)
description: Significant Data Fiduciary must appoint an independent data auditor to carry out data audit.

title: Periodic Data Protection Impact Assessment (SDF)
description: Significant Data Fiduciary must undertake periodic Data Protection Impact Assessment.

title: Periodic Audit (SDF)
description: Significant Data Fiduciary must undertake periodic audit.

title: Correction, Completion, Updating
description: Upon request, Data Fiduciary must correct inaccurate/misleading personal data, complete incomplete data, and update data.

title: Erasure upon Request
description: Upon request, Data Fiduciary must erase personal data unless retention necessary for specified purpose or compliance with law.

title: Grievance Redressal Mechanism
description: Data Fiduciary or Consent Manager must provide readily available means of grievance redressal.

title: Response to Grievances within Prescribed Period
description: Data Fiduciary or Consent Manager must respond to grievances within prescribed period (90 days per Rule 14(3)).

title: Duties of Data Principal
description: Data Principal must comply with applicable laws, not impersonate, not suppress material information, not register false grievances, and furnish verifiably authentic information.

title: Restriction on Transfer Outside India
description: Data Fiduciary must comply with any notification restricting transfer of personal data to certain countries or territories.

title: Notice Content and Presentation
description: Notice must be independently understandable, in clear and plain language, include itemised personal data and specific purpose, and provide communication link for withdrawal, rights, and complaints.

title: Registration and Obligations of Consent Manager
description: Consent Manager must register with Board, fulfil conditions in First Schedule Part A, and comply with obligations in Part B (including enabling consent management, not reading data, maintaining records for 7 years, avoiding conflict of interest, etc.).
deadline: Rule 4 comes into force 1 year after publication (Rule 1(3))
penalty_ref: Schedule - Item 6 (breach of voluntary undertaking) or Item 7
notes: Detailed obligations in First Schedule Part B.

title: Processing by State for Subsidies/Benefits
description: Processing of personal data for provision or issue of subsidy, benefit, service, certificate, licence or permit by State must follow standards in Second Schedule.

title: Reasonable Security Safeguards - Detailed Measures
description: Data Fiduciary must implement at minimum: encryption/obfuscation/masking, access controls, logs and monitoring, backups, contract provisions with Data Processor, and technical/organisational measures. Retain logs for one year.

title: Intimation of Personal Data Breach - Detailed Process
description: Data Fiduciary must intimate affected Data Principals without delay via user account or registered mode, with description, consequences, measures, safety steps, contact. Intimate Board without delay (initial) and within 72 hours (detailed report).
deadline: Without delay; detailed report within 72 hours

title: Erasure for Inactive Accounts (Specified Classes)
description: Data Fiduciary of specified classes (Third Schedule) must erase personal data after prescribed time period if Data Principal neither approaches nor exercises rights, unless retention required by law.
deadline: As per time period in Third Schedule (3 years from last approach or Rules commencement, whichever latest)

title: Prior Notice before Erasure
description: At least 48 hours before erasure, Data Fiduciary must inform Data Principal that data will be erased unless she logs in or contacts.

title: Minimum Retention of Logs
description: Data Fiduciary must retain personal data, traffic data, and logs for at least one year from processing date, then erase unless further retention required by law or notified by Government.
deadline: Retain for 1 year from processing date

title: Publishing Contact Information of Data Protection Officer
description: Data Fiduciary must prominently publish on website/app and mention in responses to rights requests the business contact info of Data Protection Officer (if applicable) or a person able to answer questions.

title: Verifiable Consent for Child - Due Diligence
description: Data Fiduciary must adopt appropriate measures to ensure verifiable consent of parent, and observe due diligence to check that parent is an identifiable adult using reliable details or voluntarily provided details/virtual token from authorised entity.

title: Verifiable Consent for Person with Disability
description: Data Fiduciary must verify that lawful guardian is appointed by court, designated authority, or local level committee under applicable law.

title: Exemptions from Child Data Obligations
description: Not applicable to specified classes of Data Fiduciaries (e.g., clinical establishments, educational institutions) and purposes (e.g., exercise of power, provision of subsidies, email account creation) subject to conditions in Fourth Schedule.

title: Annual DPIA and Audit for SDF
description: Significant Data Fiduciary must undertake a Data Protection Impact Assessment and an audit once every 12 months from date of notification.

title: Report of DPIA and Audit to Board (SDF)
description: Significant Data Fiduciary must cause the person carrying out DPIA and audit to furnish a report of significant observations to the Board.
deadline: Upon completion of each DPIA and audit

title: Algorithmic Software Diligence (SDF)
description: Significant Data Fiduciary must observe due diligence to verify that technical measures including algorithmic software are not likely to pose a risk to rights of Data Principals.
notes: "Due diligence" and "not likely to pose a risk" are subjective; ambiguous.

title: Data Localisation for Specified Personal Data (SDF)
description: Significant Data Fiduciary must ensure that personal data specified by Central Government (based on committee recommendations) is not transferred outside India.
deadline: Upon notification of specified personal data

title: Publishing Means for Exercise of Rights
description: Data Fiduciary and Consent Manager must prominently publish on website/app the means and particulars required for Data Principal to exercise rights under the Act.

title: Grievance Redressal Period
description: Data Fiduciary and Consent Manager must respond to grievances within 90 days and implement appropriate technical and organisational measures for effectiveness.

title: Restrictions on Transfer Outside India
description: Personal data may be transferred outside India subject to requirements specified by Central Government regarding making data available to foreign States or entities under their control.
notes: Requirements yet to be specified by Central Government; ambiguous.

title: Exemption for Research, Archiving, Statistical Purposes
description: Processing necessary for research, archiving or statistical purposes is exempt from the Act if carried on in accordance with standards in Second Schedule.

title: Furnishing Information to Central Government
description: Data Fiduciary or intermediary must furnish information as called for by Central Government for purposes specified in Seventh Schedule, within specified period.
notes: May be required not to disclose to affected Data Principal if prejudicial to sovereignty/security.

title: Consent Manager: Obligations (Detailed)
description: Consent Manager must: enable consent management, ensure data not readable by it, maintain records for 7 years, provide access and machine-readable format, maintain website/app, not sub-contract, take security safeguards, act in fiduciary capacity, avoid conflicts of interest, publish certain information, have audit mechanisms, and not transfer control without Board approval.
notes: See First Schedule Part B items 1-13.

title: Standards for Processing by State and for Research/Archiving
description: Processing must be: lawful, for specified uses, limited to necessary data, with reasonable efforts for completeness/accuracy/consistency, retained only as needed, with security safeguards, and for State processing also include intimation and contact details. Accountability for the person determining purpose and means.
applies_to: State, instrumentalities, and entities processing for research/archiving
